a:r.a:t: Sales | Maturity assessment
Topic
Status
HGV Observations
Initial Recommendations
Eligibility
Most sales roles on a sales compensation plans meet the three eligibility criteria
Typically, sales training roles should not be on sales compensation plans
Reassess training and other borderline roles using a consistent eligibility framework
Target Cash Compensation
Sales roles below director are generally aligned with direct competitors and vacation ownership benchmarks
Directors and above are aligned with direct competitors but shows less competitive alignment against Vacation Ownership and broader industry benchmarks
Compared to other groups, Sales EEs are more likely to feel their compensation does not reflect their efforts
Consider recalibrating leadership TCC to better align to market norms and resolve challenges with bringing in new talent at this level
Pay Mix & Leverage
HGV’s compensation is more variable-heavy than the market for most sales roles
Pay mix for sales trainers is oriented similarly to a true sales role, while in the market, this role is not typically on a sales comp plan
Rebalance pay mix to remain competitive in the market, especially as employees place greater emphasis on receiving income stability
Reassess whether the current incentive-heavy structure for sales roles is driving the intended behavior for the role
Measures & Weights
Comp plans generally use 2-4 measures
Core sales plans commonly use sales volume, which aligns to core commercial outcomes; management level plans include profitability measures
Current measures may underweight customer experience (customer satisfaction is a common measure for competitors)
Simplify payout curves and reduce number of tiers
Standardize core plan mechanics across similar sales roles while preserving targeted local flexibility only where justified
Compensation Ranges
Currently unable to assess with the data received
Unable to fully assess compensation ranges with the data provided at this time
Payment Periods
Marketing comp plan periods are mostly set to monthly cycles, aligning to the high volume and short sales cycle focus of the industry
Commissions payouts are made on a biweekly basis, with monthly or quarterly bonuses based on other measures
N/A – Payment cycles generally align to market practice
Quota Setting
In some cases, monthly adjustments are required, which may reduce stability and confidence in targets
Sales EEs are less likely to view their quota targets as fair compared to other groups
Establish a more consistent, data-driven quota-setting process with defined owners and timelines
Reduce routine target resets and govern exceptions through a formal adjustment process
SPIFFs
HGV has formal SPIFF policy guardrails, including approval and payroll processing requirements and are used as supplemental short-term incentives (site-specific)
Continue to use SPIFFs selectively for targeted short-term behavior change, not as a substitute for base plan design
Strengthen governance and tracking to ensure SPIFF usage remains controlled, measurable, and aligned to business priorities across all sites
Telemarketing
Marketing
Sales
Limited Issues / Minor Opportunities for Improvement
Moderate Issues / Clear Opportunity for Improvement
Significant Issues Discovered / Action Required to Address Gaps
Insufficient Data Available
Compensation Design & Market Assessment
